news.title: IBM buys HRL Laboratories in shift to two-track quantum computing strategy
news.summary: IBM has agreed to buy HRL Laboratories, a private quantum computing research lab jointly owned by Boeing Co and General Motors, the ‌company said on Thursday, adding a second pillar to IBM's quantum computing efforts.  IBM has been racing ‌against Alphabet's Google, Microsoft and others to create practical quantum machines that can crack problems that would take conventional computers thousands ​of years to solve.  In May, U.S. President Donald Trump's administration said it would award $1 billion to IBM to set up a new company called Anderon in New Albany, New York, to serve as a chip factory for U.S. quantum computing firms.

news.title: Oil prices extend rally as Red Sea attacks push Brent above $97 per barrel
news.summary: Oil prices advanced for a fifth consecutive session on Thursday after Houthi forces claimed attacks on two Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea, intensifying concerns over the security of key global energy shipping routes and lifting Brent crude above $97 a barrel. Brent crude futures for September delivery rose 3.

news.title: Gold steadies near two-week high as investors monitor Fed outlook and oil prices

news.title: Alphabet Stock Is Falling as AI Spending Continues to Mount
news.summary: Alphabet  reported blowout earnings results late Wednesday, but the stock is dropping after the search giant again raised its forecast for capital expenditures.  The Google-parent reported second-quarter adjusted earnings of $9.11 per share, well ahead of Wall Street’s consensus estimate for earnings of $2.88 per share, according to FactSet.  Alphabet said the results included $6.26 a share in gains on the fair value of its equity securities.

news.title: Stocks to Watch: Alphabet, Tesla, Nestle, Intel
news.summary: ↘️ Tesla (TSLA): Elon Musk’s EV maker reported negative free cash flow of more than $1 billion in the second quarter amid heavy spending on AI and robotics. Shares fell about 5% premarket. ↘️ Alphabet (GOOGL): Shares slid more than 3% premarket after the tech company announced plans to significantly boost its capital expenditure.
